heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the inside temperature (up 2.2°C), cooler for the outside temperature (down 1.7°C), and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 6%).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1681 m), feels like temperature (down 1.8°C), and dew point temperature (down 3.8°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 494.9m lower than yesterday, 1681.3m lower than the past week, 1458.9m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 814m to a maximum of 3430m, averaging 1332.0m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 3010m and 2790m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, air moisture should feel ok and little or no wind. Possible light showers, partial cloudiness with some periods of sun, and the light is absent. Changeable conditions.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (86%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
